overview.short: Saptashrungi or Saptashringi (Marathi: सप्तशृङ्गि, Saptaśr̥ṇgī) is a site of Hindu pilgrimage in the village of Vani, situated 60 kilometres (37 mi) from Nashik in Indian state of
overview.description: Saptashrungi or Saptashringi (Marathi: सप्तशृङ्गि, Saptaśr̥ṇgī) is a site of Hindu pilgrimage in the village of Vani, situated 60 kilometres (37 mi) from Nashik in Indian state of Maharashtra. According to Hindu traditions, the goddess Saptashrungi Nivasini dwells within the seven mountain peaks. (Sapta means seven and shrung means peaks.) It is located in Nanduri, Kalwan taluka, a small village near Nashik in India. The Marathas and some Hindu tribes have worshipped the goddess from a long time and some worship her as their kuldaivat.

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Satmala Range
topPlaces.description: Satmala (pronunciation:[sɐtmaːla]) is a mountain range which runs across Nashik District, Maharashtra. They are an integral part of the Sahyadris range within Nashik.

topPlaces.name: Nanduri Dam
topPlaces.description: Nanduri Dam, is an earthfill and gravity dam on Tapti river near Ama Local Nallaher, Nashik district in the state of Maharashtra in India.

topPlaces.name: Javlya fort
topPlaces.description: Javlya Fort (Marathi: जवळ्या किल्ला, Hindi: जवळ्या किला, (transliteration: Marathi-"Javḷyā Killā and Hindi: Javḷyā Kilā or Qilā) is located 43 km from Nashik, Nashik district, of Maharashtra. Javlya fort and Ravlya fort are twin forts located on a single hill plateau, Ravlya on the west and Javlya on the east side of the hill plateau.

topPlaces.name: Ahivant Fort
topPlaces.description: Ahivant Fort is a fort located 55km from Nashik, in Nashik district, of Maharashtra. This fort is an important fort in Nashik district.

topPlaces.name: Dhunda fort
topPlaces.description: Dhunda Fort (Marathi: धुंदा किल्ला, Hindi: धुंदया किला, transliteration: Dhundya Qilа̄)is located 106 km from Nashik, Nashik district, of Maharashtra.The Dhundya fort is located on a single hillock west of the road from Malegaon to Satana.

topPlaces.name: Ravlya Fort
topPlaces.description: Ravalya Fort (Marathi: रवळ्या किल्ला, Hindi: रवल्या किला, transliteration: Ravalya Qilа̄)is located 43 km from Nashik, Nashik district, of Maharashtra. The Ravlya fort and Javlya fort are twin forts located on a single hill plateau.

topPlaces.name: Punegaon Dam
topPlaces.description: Punegaon Dam, is an earthfill dam on Unanda river near Punegaon, Vani, Nashik district in the state of Maharashtra in India.

topPlaces.name: Abhona
topPlaces.description: Abhona is a village in Kalwan tehsil of Nashik district in the Indian state of Maharashtra. It belongs to the North Maharashtra region.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Saptashrungi?
faq.a: The best time to visit Saptashrungi is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

The nearest airport is Shirdi Airport (92 km) and the nearest railway station is Nashik Road (50 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Saptashrungi?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Saptashrungi at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Saptashrungi?
faq.a: Stays in Saptashrungi start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Saptashrungi?
faq.a: The top attractions are Satmala Range, Nanduri Dam, Javlya fort, Ahivant Fort, Dhunda fort.
